verses.t: These are the words of the book which Baruch the son of Neraiah, son of Mahseiah, son of Zedekiah, son of Hasadiah, son of Hilkiah, wrote in Babylon,
in the fifth year, on the seventh day of the month, at the time when the Chaldeans took Yerushalayim and burned it with fire.
And Baruch read the words of this book in the hearing of Jeconiah the son of Jehoiakim, king of Yahuḏah, and in the hearing of all the people who came to hear the book,
and in the hearing of the mighty men and the princes, and in the hearing of the Alders, and in the hearing of all the people, small and great, all who dwelt in Babylon by the river Sud.
Then they wept, and fasted, and prayed before Yahuah;
and they collected money, each giving what he could;
and they sent it to Yerushalayim to Jehoiakim the kohen gadol, the son of Hilkiah, son of Shallum, and to the kohanim, and to all the people who were present with him in Yerushalayim.

At the same time, on the tenth day of Sivan, Baruch took the vessels of the house of Yahuah, which had been carried away from the heykal, to return them to the land of Yahuḏah -- the silver vessels which Zedekiah the son of Josiah, king of Yahuḏah, had made,
after Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon had carried away from Yerushalayim Jeconiah and the princes and the prisoners and the mighty men and the people of the land, and brought them to Babylon.

And they said: "Herewith we send you money; so buy with the money burnt offerings and sin offerings and incense, and prepare a cereal offering, and offer them upon the mizbe'ach of Yahuah our Aluahim;
and pray for the life of Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon, and for the life of Belshazzar his son, that their days on earth may be like the days of shamayim.

And Yahuah will give us strength, and he will give light to our eyes, and we shall live under the protection of Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon, and under the protection of Belshazzar his son, and we shall serve them many days and find chen in their sight.
And pray for us to Yahuah our Aluahim, for we have sinned against Yahuah our Aluahim, and to this day the anger of Yahuah and his wrath have not

in the fifth year, on the seventh day of the month, at the time when the Chaldeans took Yerushalayim and burned it with fire.
And Baruch read the words of this book in the hearing of Jeconiah the son of Jehoiakim, king of Yahuḏah, and in the hearing of all the people who came to hear the book,
and in the hearing of the mighty men and the princes, and in the hearing of the Alders, and in the hearing of all the people, small and great, all who dwelt in Babylon by the river Sud.
Then they wept, and fasted, and prayed before Yahuah;
and they collected money, each giving what he could;
and they sent it to Yerushalayim to Jehoiakim the kohen gadol, the son of Hilkiah, son of Shallum, and to the kohanim, and to all the people who were present with him in Yerushalayim.

At the same time, on the tenth day of Sivan, Baruch took the vessels of the house of Yahuah, which had been carried away from the heykal, to return them to the land of Yahuḏah -- the silver vessels which Zedekiah the son of Josiah, king of Yahuḏah, had made,
after Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon had carried away from Yerushalayim Jeconiah and the princes and the prisoners and the mighty men and the people of the land, and brought them to Babylon.

And they said: "Herewith we send you money; so buy with the money burnt offerings and sin offerings and incense, and prepare a cereal offering, and offer them upon the mizbe'ach of Yahuah our Aluahim;
and pray for the life of Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon, and for the life of Belshazzar his son, that their days on earth may be like the days of shamayim.

And Yahuah will give us strength, and he will give light to our eyes, and we shall live under the protection of Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon, and under the protection of Belshazzar his son, and we shall serve them many days and find chen in their sight.
And pray for us to Yahuah our Aluahim, for we have sinned against Yahuah our Aluahim, and to this day the anger of Yahuah and his wrath have not turned away from us.
And you shall read this book which we are sending you, to make your confession in the house of Yahuah on the days of the feasts and at appointed seasons.
"And you shall say: 'Righteousness belongs to Yahuah our Aluahim, but confusion of face, as at this day, to us, to the men of Yahuḏah, to the inhabitants of Yerushalayim,
and to our kings and our princes and our kohanim and our neḇi'im and our fathers,
because we have sinned before Yahuah,
and have disobeyed him, and have not heeded the voice of Yahuah our Aluahim, to walk in the statutes of Yahuah which he set before us.
From the day when Yahuah brought our fathers out of the land of Mitsrayim until today, we have been disobedient to Yahuah our Aluahim, and we have been negligent, in not heeding his voice.

So to this day there have clung to us the calamities and the curse which Yahuah declared through Mosheh his servant at the time when he brought our fathers out of the land of Mitsrayim to give to us a land flowing with milk and honey.

We did not heed the voice of Yahuah our Aluahim in all the words of the neḇi'im whom he sent to us, but we each followed the intent of his own wicked heart by serving other Aluahims and doing what is evil in the sight of Yahuah our Aluahim.
But if you will not obey the voice of Yahuah and will not serve the king of Babylon,

I will make to cease from the cities of Yahuḏah and from the region about Yerushalayim the voice of mirth and the voice of gladness, the voice of the bridegroom and the voice of the bride, and the whole land will be a desolation without inhabitants."

"'But we did not obey thy voice, to serve the king of Babylon; and thou hast confirmed thy words, which thou didst speak by thy servants the neḇi'im, that the bones of our kings and the bones of our fathers would be brought out of their graves;
and behold, they have been cast out to the heat of day and the frost of night. They perished in great misery, by famine and sword and pestilence.
And the house which is called by thy name thou hast made as it is today, because of the wickedness of the house of Yasharal and the house of Yahuḏah.
"'Yet thou hast dealt with us, 0 Yahuah our Aluahim, in all thy kindness and in all thy great compassion,
as thou didst speak by thy servant Mosheh on the day when thou didst command him to write thy law in the presence of the people of Yasharal, saying,
"If you will not obey my voice, this very great multitude will surely turn into a small number among the nations, where I will scatter them.
For I know that they will not obey me, for they are a stiff-necked people. But in the land of their exile they will come to themselves,
and they will know that I am Yahuah their Aluahim. I will give them a heart that obeys and ears that hear;
and they will praise me in the land of their exile, and will remember my name,
and will turn from their stubbornness and their wicked deeds; for they will remember the ways of their fathers, who sinned before Yahuah.

I will bring them again into the land which I swore to give to their fathers, to Aḇraham and to Yitsḥaq and to Ya'aqoḇ, and they will rule over it; and I will increase them, and they will not be diminished.
I will make an everlasting berith with them to be their Aluahim and they shall be my people; and I will never again remove my people Yasharal from the land which I have given them."
He found the whole way to knowledge, and gave her to Ya'aqoḇ his servant and to Yasharal whom he loved.
Afterward 21 6 she appeared upon earth and lived among men.
